notes: KNOWN FAILING — irrlicht issue #169. After /clear, claudecode opens UUID-2's transcript cleanly but the daemon NEVER emits transcript_removed for UUID-1. The spec models the pre-session proc-PID → UUID-1 → /clear → UUID-2 lifecycle precisely now: session_birth matches the proc-PID's ready (first event), v1_session_handoff matches the UUID-keyed ready, all v1 phases are pinned to v1_session_handoff via same_session_as, old_session_ended requires transcript_removed for THAT exact UUID (rejecting the pre-session removal as a false positive), and v2_session_birth requires new_session: true — the strongest observable proof of 'content was reset'. Failure attribution after the rewrite: 'no transcript_removed found for session <UUID-1>' — the actionable signal for fixing the daemon-side emission. Drop the flag once the daemon emits transcript_removed for the cleared UUID.

text: Pre-session row (proc-<PID>) appears in ready within 1s
After the first prompt lands, the UUID-keyed session appears in ready — proc-<PID> hands off to a fresh UUID
First user prompt drives ready → working on the UUID session
First turn completes back to ready (same UUID)
Spec: 'Old session disappears' — after /clear, the daemon must emit transcript_removed for the FIRST UUID (NOT for the pre-session proc-<PID>)
Spec: 'New session appears in ready with a NEW session_id' — a fresh UUID never seen before; this is the strongest observable proxy for 'content was reset'
Second user prompt drives the NEW session ready → working (must NOT match a stale working transition on UUID-1)
Second turn completes back to ready on the new session
